gloss: long ago, for a long time
help! (exclamation) (archaic; conservative Karelian/coastal register — std. helpô)
darling, love (archaic; conservative Karelian/coastal register — std. estimô)
strawberry (cf. berry marjô; the cultivated kind)
filth-sect; insult for an out-group denomination (slur; coarse/polemical)
devil-market; insult for a corrupt or drunken church fair (folk/polemical; coarse)
cream-filled pastry (Belgian import); (by extension) any donut or filled sweet dough
hell-bell, death bell rung for plague, wreck, or execution (folk fear; coarse)
faith, religious faith (Romance/Latin; institutional, cf. ūsk 'belief')
the lead, front position (in a race or contest) (late-stratum)
yarn, spun thread (Low German; cf. native lông)
grave lamp; lamp left at graves or wreck memorials (grave ritual)
help! (exclamation; Low German panic-cry — distinct from the verb abīstā 'to help', which stays Finnic)
fine, okay (conceding); yeah (archaic; conservative Karelian/coastal register — std. gūd)
icon-queue; the long line before an icon or relic shrine (Orthodox-border slang)
dear, beloved (archaic; conservative Karelian/coastal register — std. lēvü)
fish-pardon; paying the church in fish to settle a small sin or fee (folk/port, mocking)
cross-water, holy water (folk term; formal is sakrôveç)
sweetie, dearie (archaic; conservative Karelian/coastal register — std. kohanü)
hey, listen (archaic; conservative Karelian/coastal register — std. lüstä)
to converse, speak back and forth
to fall, drop (of prices, water, level)
to lower, bring down (causative of langô)
go on, go ahead, let’s do it
to set (of the sun); to sink
market (Low German trade-word; cf. turg "market square")
black book; parish debt/scandal/excommunication register (parish bureaucracy)
afterworld, land of the dead (Slavic/older death-lore)
just now, soon, in a bit
fine, all right, so be it
please (archaic; conservative Karelian/coastal register — std. bidô)
a heavy or stiff coat (ironic; lit. 'armor', cf. harniskô) (Romance)
prayer-grandmother; an old woman paid to pray, mourn, or remember the dead (folk/religious)
percent (formal term; per hundred, the hundred a full gross)
border rite; mixed Latin/Orthodox/local rite of the border towns (frontier religion)
medicine, medication (cf. rohô "grass"; the healing-herb sense)
shore spirit; spirit of beaches and drowned sailors (folk religion)
shore pilgrim; one who walks the coast shrines (folk pilgrimage)
cheers, here you go (a toast; Scandinavian)
beach, waterfront (the newer, nautical word; cf. rānd)
salt-cross, cross marked with salt before sailing or oaths (sailor folk-religion)
to weave (Low German; cf. native kudā)
welcome (archaic; conservative Karelian/coastal register — std. velkô)
here you go; cheers (archaic; conservative Karelian/coastal register — std. skōl)
to greet (archaic; conservative Karelian/coastal register — std. grȫtä)
to hem, sew an edge (Low German)
thank you (archaic; conservative Karelian/coastal register — std. dankô)
hello (archaic; conservative Karelian/coastal register — std. moin)
toll-priest, fee-taking priest (hostile/folk; dockside insult for clergy who sell blessings)
water spirit; a shore or river spirit (folk religion)
to conquer, take by force (Low German)
to affect, have effect on (Low German)
ship-mass, dockside mass before departure (port ritual)
to bustle, make street noise (Marīsô, slav)
heavy boot, work or riding boot (Marīsô, slav)
